user_prompt: Describe Bernoulli’s principle through an airplane wing example in depth.

style_bible.visual_style: Clean scientific visualization blended with cinematic realism, semi-transparent airflow graphics over a realistic airplane wing.
style_bible.camera_rules: Smooth, deliberate movements; no abrupt motion; prioritize clarity of airflow and pressure visuals.
style_bible.lighting_rules: Bright neutral daylight with soft highlights; airflow and pressure graphics glow subtly without overpowering.
style_bible.continuity_rules: Airplane wing orientation, airflow direction, and graphic color coding remain consistent across all shots and clips.

clips.clip_script: Introduce the airplane wing and show how incoming air divides and accelerates over the curved top surface.

shots.framing: Wide side view of an airplane wing cutting through the sky
shots.subject_action: The wing moves steadily forward as faint airflow lines approach the leading edge.
shots.continuity_note: Airflow lines continue forming along the wing surface into the next shot.
shots.lighting: Bright daylight with soft reflections on the wing

shots.framing: Medium close-up of the wing’s cross-section
shots.subject_action: Airflow lines speed up over the curved top and slow underneath.
shots.continuity_note: Speed difference between upper and lower airflow remains visible for the next shot.

shots.framing: Angled close-up emphasizing top vs bottom airflow
shots.subject_action: Upper airflow stretches and thins while lower airflow remains denser.
shots.continuity_note: FINAL FRAME HANDOFF: Wing frozen mid-flight with airflow lines active, ready to introduce pressure difference.
shots.cinematic_look: Smooth cinematic motion with clean overlays

clips.negative_prompt: logos, watermarks, brand marks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch, people, human, face, hands, cockpit, unreadable tiny text

clips.clip_script: Build on the airflow to explain pressure differences and show how lift is produced.

shots.framing: Same wing angle as previous final frame
shots.subject_action: Pressure shading appears above and below the wing.
shots.continuity_note: Begins exactly from Clip 1 final frame; pressure colors intensify into the next shot.
shots.lighting: Neutral daylight with colored pressure glow

shots.framing: Clean cross-sectional diagram view of the wing
shots.subject_action: Arrows illustrate pressure pushing up from below.
shots.continuity_note: Pressure arrows remain in place as lift visualization begins next.

shots.framing: Three-quarter view of wing gaining upward motion
shots.subject_action: The wing subtly rises as lift force arrows glow.
shots.continuity_note: FINAL FRAME HANDOFF: Wing stabilized in lifted position with airflow and pressure visuals unified.

clips.negative_prompt: logos, watermarks, brand marks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch, people, human, face, hands, cockpit, unreadable tiny text

full_script_20s: A realistic airplane wing in flight is shown. Airflow lines reveal how air splits above and below the wing. The video slows to explain that faster airflow above the wing creates lower pressure. The difference between upper and lower pressure is visualized, leading to the upward lift force that keeps the airplane aloft.
